Reasoning
Q1
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting
around a rectangular table in such a way three
persons are sitting on the each longer side of
the table and one person is sitting on the each
shorter side of the table. All of them are facing
outside the table. All the information is not
necessarily in the same order.
D sits third to the left of H and sits opposite to
A. A and C sit near to each other. C does not sit
on the shorter side of the table. There are two
people sitting between C and G. The number of
people sitting between G and H is the same
number of people sitting between G and B. F
sits third to the left of E.
Four of the following five are alike in a certain
way and so form a group. Which one does not
belong to that group?
(A) D-B
(B) H-F
(C) E-D
(D) G-F
(E) None of these

Study the following information and answer

shaped table facing inwards. One person is
sitting at one corner each and two people are
sitting along each side. No one among Q, U, or
Z is sitting at the corner of the table. Only one
person is sitting between P and S. O is sitting
third to the right of N and is sitting second to
the left of V. The number of people sitting
between U and Q when counted from the left
of Q is not more than three. Only three people
are sitting between P and Y, who is not sitting
along the longer side of the table. Five people
are sitting between S and R, who is sitting third
to the right of T. Only one person is sitting
between Y and N, who is not sitting along the
longer side of the table. X is sitting in one of the
corners of the table and is sitting immediately
to the left of R. The number of people sitting
between Z and Q is one more than the number
of people sitting between X and O.
Which of the following statements is correct?
(A) Five people are sitting between T and Q.

(B) Z and R are sitting adjacent to each other.
(C) S is sitting third to the left of Q.
(D) Four people are sitting between P and O.
(E) Both (a) and (d)
